items.title: About 50 principal, headmaster posts filled - Fadhlina
items.summary: IPOH: The Education Ministry has filled about 50 vacant headmaster and principal posts nationwide in a move aimed at strengthening school administration and addressing urgent operational and safety concerns. Its minister Fadhlina Sidek said schools cannot function effectively without strong leadership, stressing that principals and headmasters are essential in building a stable school ecosystem. She said the vacancies were identified as part of ongoing efforts to reform school administration and improve daily management, particularly in relation to safety and governance issues.

items.title: Sunway issues tranche 1 series 1 Sukuk Wakalah worth US$5 mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Bhd has issued its first issuance of Sukuk Wakalah (Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah) amounting to US$5 million (US$1=RM3.95). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, it said proceeds raised from the issuance of Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah shall be utilised to finance Shariah-compliant investment activities, capital expenditure, working capital requirements and/or other general corporate purposes of Sunway Group. HSBC Amanah Malaysia Bhd is the sole lead manager for the issuance of Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah.

items.title: Carlsberg net profit rises to RM98mil, declares 24 sen dividend in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd's net profit rose 4.7 per cent to RM98.94 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), from RM94.52 million a year earlier, on the back of higher revenue. Quarterly revenue rose 6.5 per cent to RM705.95 million from RM662.81 million previously, the company's filing to Bursa Malaysia showed. According to Carlsberg, its Malaysia operations delivered revenue growth of 9.6 per cent and a 2.9 per cent increase in profit from operations, driven by festive demand and effective market execution.

items.title: Case closed: MACC classifies IJM chairman probe as NFA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has classified investigations involving IJM Corporation Bhd non-executive chairman Tan Sri Krishnan Tan as no further action (NFA). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the anti-graft agency had confirmed that no prosecution would be pursued in relation to investigations linked to the voluntary takeover offer by Sunway Bhd. "The company has cooperated fully with MACC throughout the process and remained committed to supporting the authorities as required," IJM said.

items.title: 80pct of teacher transfer cases involving spouses resolved - Fadhlina
items.summary: IPOH: Teacher welfare, particularly involving long-distance postings and transfer applications, continues to be a priority for the Education Ministry. She added that cases involving long-distance spouse postings make up the majority of transfer applications handled by the ministry. She said about 80 per cent of transfer applications involve long-distance spouse cases, and the ministry managed to resolve these.

items.title: Sunway REIT appoints Derek Teh as CEO effective Dec 1, 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Real Estate Investment Trust (Sunway REIT) has appointed Derek Teh Wan Wei as chief executive officer (CEO) of Sunway REIT Management Sdn Bhd, effective Dec 1, 2025. In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, Sunway REIT said Teh, 48, currently serves as Malaysian REIT Managers Association's vice-chairman. Before joining Sunway REIT Management, he was Sentral REIT Management Sdn Bhd CEO.

items.title: Education Ministry fills 50 school leadership posts, says vacancies will be addressed promptly
items.summary: Its Minister, Fadhlina Sidek, said the ministry views the issue seriously, stressing that schools cannot function effectively without strong leadership. She said strong leadership is crucial in implementing school reforms, ensuring safety, and managing daily school operations. She said the appointments involved schools in every state and took effect immediately.

items.title: Petaling Jaya activates flood sirens as Sungai Penchala water level rises
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Flood warning sirens were activated in several areas around Petaling Jaya today after continuous heavy rain caused water levels at Sungai Penchala to rise to dangerous levels. As a precautionary measure, the flood warning system was activated to alert residents living near affected areas. The latest warning comes amid growing concern over flash floods in Petaling Jaya following several recent incidents caused by prolonged heavy rain.

items.title: Police probe Bedong school fight involving female students
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Police have identified between six and 10 female students believed to be involved in a fight in front of a secondary school in Bedong, which went viral on social media since yesterday. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the incident involved secondary school students from Form One to Form Four, but initial investigations found that only two or three students were directly involved in the fight. "Our review of the viral video found that the actions of several students attempting to break up the fight created the impression that everyone at the scene was involved in the incident," he told reporters here, today.

items.title: Anwar: Move to revoke heritage players' citizenship must follow due process
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Any move to revoke the citizenship of the seven suspended Harimau Malaya heritage players must go through the proper process, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar was responding to questions on proposals to revoke the citizenship of the seven suspended Harimau Malaya heritage players. Earlier, FAM suffered a major blow when seven heritage players who joined the Harimau Malaya squad in 2025 were suspended for 12 months after being found to be involved in issues related to document forgery.

items.title: MACC clears Krishnan Tan of wrongdoing, IJM chairman says he wants to move on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Tan Sri Krishnan Tan today confirmed that the Malaysian Anti‑Corruption Commission (MACC) has formally cleared him of wrongdoing, following the conclusion of investigations into the sale of IJM Corporation Berhad shares to Sunway Berhad. In a statement today, the IJM Corporation Berhad chairman said he welcomed the decision, adding that he had “steadfastly maintained” his innocence. Previously, IJM Corporation Berhad had clarified that it is not being investigated as a corporate entity, and that MACC’s probe was confined to several individuals linked to the group.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends lower, tracking weak regional sentiment
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank fell eight sen to RM11.10 and CIMB lost two sen to RM7.88, while Public Bank added eight sen to RM4.91, Tenaga Nasional rose six sen to RM14.54 and IHH Healthcare gained two sen to RM9.04. On the most active list, AirAsia X slipped seven sen to RM1.15, Top Glove decreased five sen to 78 sen and Genetec eased half a sen to 32.5 sen, while IRIS jumped 9.5 sen to 27.5 sen and GIIB was one sen firmer at 29 sen. Among the top gainers, MKH jumped 33 sen to RM1.46, Fraser & Neave advanced 30 sen to RM29.0, United Plantations was 28 sen firmer at RM30.26 and Lianson widened 23 sen to RM1.78.

items.title: #NSTviral: Japanese tourist's bus experience raises questions on Visit Malaysia campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing a Japanese tourist being called out by a bus driver after allegedly failing to pay her fare has sparked debate online over the friendliness of Malaysia's public transport system towards foreign visitors ahead of Visit Malaysia Year. The clip quickly gained traction online, with many netizens debating whether Malaysia's public transport payment system was convenient for tourists. Another user, @UntoldHero, said Malaysia's public transport system was still not tourist-friendly.

items.title: Govt launches real-time dashboard to track global supply crisis
items.summary: In a Facebook post today, the ministry said the Global Supply Crisis Monitoring Dashboard represents a new government approach to crisis management using near-real-time data. Available data includes current fuel prices, petroleum trade performance, global energy prices, national economic indicators, currency exchange trends and commodity trade analytics. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: May 26 verdict on Hydroshoppe's corruption case transfer bid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court here today set May 26 to deliver its decision on an application by Hydroshoppe Sdn Bhd and its director to transfer a corruption case from the Sessions Court to the High Court. Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin fixed the date after hearing submissions from Hydroshoppe and Datuk Abdul Hamid Shaikh Abdul Razak Shaikh's lawyer, Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, and deputy public prosecutor Farah Ezlin Yusop Khan. Earlier, Amer Hamzah explained that the charges involve two separate entities: one against Abdul Hamid Shaikh as an individual under Section 16(b)(B) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 and another against the company under Section 17A(1)(a) of the same Act.

items.title: Homeowner, swimming club in Ipoh settle pickleball noise row with indoor facility plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — A homeowner and the Ipoh Swimming Club have reached a settlement in a lawsuit over noise from an outdoor pickleball court. The Ipoh High Court recorded a consent judgment before judge Moses Susayan, with the club agreeing that no open‑air pickleball games would be allowed in the area adjoining the house, as reported by Free Malaysia Today. Cheang had filed the suit earlier this year, claiming the club built a pickleball court next to his home on Lorong Gopeng at Taman Golf.

items.title: Sepang cop died of heart disease during marching drill, post-mortem confirms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A post-mortem conducted on a police corporal who collapsed during a marching drill at the Sepang district police headquarters on Wednesday confirmed that his death was caused by coronary atherosclerosis, or heart disease. Sepang police chief Assistant Commissioner Norhizam Bahaman said the 37-year-old corporal, attached to the Dato' Abu Bakar Baginda police station, died during an additional marching exercise conducted at the headquarters parade ground at about 4pm on May 13. "Sepang police deny baseless allegations claiming the incident involved abuse.

items.title: India's Ola Electric to invest US$208.5mil in EV, cell tech units
items.summary: BENGALURU: India's Ola Electric will invest US$208.5 million in its core vehicle and cell units as the EV bike and scooter maker aims to step up cost cuts and localise manufacturing to achieve profitability amid rising competition. The firm also plans to launch a new cost-efficient line of EV two-wheeler models. Its EV unit posted a revenue of 47.17 billion rupees for the year ended March 31, 2026, while its cell unit posted a revenue of 730 million rupees.

items.title: BYD to work with Malaysian govt on EV future
items.summary: BYD Co Ltd has said that it remains committed to investing in Malaysia amid uncertainty of the Chinese carmaker's future after the government tightens rules on fully imported (CBU) electric vehicles. BYD vice president Liu Xueliang said it will continue working closely with the government, distributors and dealer partners to develop new energy vehicle (NEV) solutions that are better suited to the local market. The new requirements follow the expiry of Malaysia's special EV import exemption scheme at the end of 2025.

items.title: Kara Barista Coconut Milk shines at ICBS 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kara Malaysia reinforced its position in the local café industry at the International Café & Beverage Show (ICBS) 2026, taking on an expanded role as official barista coconut milk sponsor and on-ground branding partner. Backed by more than 25 years of coconut expertise, Kara Malaysia showcased its "From Tree To Cup" philosophy, highlighting how its coconut milk is developed specifically for barista use. Kara Barista Coconut Milk was also presented across a range of applications, from hot and iced coffee to tea-based beverages and desserts, reflecting its versatility in modern café menus.

items.title: PM urges efficient delivery of 13MP projects for people's benefit
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for all projects under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) to be implemented efficiently and closely monitored to ensure they genuinely benefit the people. He said discussions focused on areas closely linked to the people's needs, including education, flood mitigation, infrastructure, healthcare and socio-economic strengthening. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said project monitoring must be strengthened to prevent delays, leakages or implementation issues that could affect the people.

items.title: [UPDATED] Ex-driver charged with criminally intimidating High Court judge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former driver pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate's Court here today to a charge of criminally intimidating a High Court judge he once served. Mohd Fareez Mohd Zin, 39, was accused of criminally intimidating High Court civil judge Gan Techiong, 64, with the intention of causing fear and distress. The alleged offence took place at 1.30pm at Gan's court complex in Jalan Duta on May 8.

items.title: Cops record Pas MP's statement over controversial ceramah remarks
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Police today recorded a statement from Datuk Dr Nik Muhammad Zawawi Salleh following his controversial remarks made during a recent ceramah. Kota Baru district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri confirmed the matter when contacted. He said police had recorded Nik Zawawi's statement to facilitate investigations into the case.

items.title: Palestine embassy urges global action, accountability on Nakba anniversary
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR:The Embassy of Palestine in Malaysia has urged the international community to uphold international law and ensure accountability over continuing violations against Palestinians. In a statement issued in conjunction with the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, the embassy said the destruction unfolding in the occupied Palestinian territories was a pattern of violations in flagrant breach of international humanitarian law and binding international resolutions. It also called for an end to the entrenched system of impunity and urged the international community to hold Israel fully accountable for its continuing crimes, including war crimes, crimes against humanity and genocide.

items.title: 32 foreign women fined RM1,000 each over passport offences
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Thirty-two foreign women were fined RM1,000 each by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to violating conditions related to their passports earlier this month. He is expected to be charged under Section 55E of the Immigration Act 1959/63. In the same court, two other foreign women claimed trial to overstaying in Malaysia without reasonable excuse.

items.title: Russia, Ukraine swap 205 prisoners of war each
items.summary: KYIV: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 205 prisoners of war each today, Moscow and Kyiv said, a week after US President Donald Trump announced a large swap would take place between the warring sides. The Russian defence ministry said in a statement on social media that "205 Russian servicemen were returned from territory" controlled by Kyiv, adding that, "in exchange, 205 Ukrainian armed forces prisoners of war were transferred." Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Telegram most of the Ukrainians handed over had been in Russian captivity since 2022.

items.title: Ex-polytechnic student walks free after judge bins 3.4kg drug trafficking charge, says key witness not credible
items.summary: Judge Datuk Azhar Abdul Hamid made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against Harith Izzuddin Mohd Hanizam, 23, at the end of its case. In delivering his decision, Azhar said the court found that the prosecution had relied on the testimony of the fourth prosecution witness (SP4), the complainant who is also a police officer, to prove the element of possession. “Therefore, I ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case against the accused.

items.title: [UPDATED] Applications for non-national stream graduates to open on June 30
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Applications for students from outside the national education system to enter public higher education institutions will open on June 30. Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said applications would first be handled directly by the respective universities before being made available through the UPUOnline system next year. For Chinese language-related degree programmes, Azlinda said four programmes were currently available in three universities.

items.title: Entry standards for non-national stream leavers cannot be lowered, says ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Entry standards and quality for school leavers from outside the national education system into public higher education institutions cannot be compromised, the Higher Education Ministry said today. Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said admission requirements would remain under the authority of each university senate, which determines academic standards and quality. Her remarks came after the United Chinese School Committees' Association of Malaysia (Dong Zong) criticised the government's new conditional pathway for students outside the national education system to enter public universities, saying the admission requirements remained restrictive.

items.title: From service to second career: WBL programme helps military veterans build future prospects
items.summary: MASJID TANAH, May 15 — The Defence Ministry is expanding its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme to help military personnel secure better-paying and more stable jobs after retirement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said many Armed Forces personnel enter service with only the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification and, without intervention, retire with the same academic credentials despite accumulating decades of skills and experience during their service. “Most military personnel join the Armed Forces with only an SPM qualification.

items.title: MACC investigates alleged multi-million ringgit bill of lading discrepancies at Tanjung Langsat Port in Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is investigating alleged discrepancies between bills of lading (BL) documentation and the actual physical movement of cargo within the petroleum supply chain at Tanjung Langsat Port in Johor. Sources said the probe centres on alleged inconsistencies involving shipping documentation and cargo handling linked to transactions amounting to several million ringgit. The development comes amid growing concerns within the maritime and commodity trading industry over mismatches between shipping documentation and actual cargo movement.

items.title: Home minister says drug problem in Langkawi real, but not like Pablo Escobar’s cartel
items.summary: KULIM, May 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has denied a news report that likened drug trafficking activities in Langkawi to the operations of an international cartel. According to him, the magnitude of drug trafficking on the resort island cannot be likened to the drug cartel led by drug trafficker Pablo Escobar who controls the entire drug trafficking complex. Commenting on the proposal of Kedah Menteri Besar, Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor for the police to shoot drug dealer, Saifuddin Nasution said the drug issue needs to be addressed rationally.

items.title: Ministry probes petrol station over alleged RON95 sale to foreign-registered vehicle
items.summary: BERUAS: The Perak Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has launched an investigation into a petrol station here following allegations that a foreign-registered vehicle attempted to purchase subsidised RON95 petrol using deceptive tactics to evade detection. He said an investigation paper had been opened under Regulation 3(2) of the Supply Control Regulations (Prohibition on the Sale and Purchase of Controlled Goods) (RON95 Petrol) 2026 for allegedly breaching Regulation 3(1), which prohibits the sale of RON95 petrol for use in foreign-registered vehicles without written permission from the Controller. Another investigation is also being conducted under Regulation 4(3) of the same regulations for allegedly breaching Regulation 4(1), which prohibits the purchase of RON95 petrol for use in foreign-registered vehicles without written approval.

items.title: Govt focus on reforms strengthens Malaysia's economic momentum – Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government's focus on MADANI Economy reforms, anchored on pro-growth policies, people-centric programmes and responsible fiscal management, has continued to strengthen Malaysia's economic momentum, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said this approach has helped Malaysia build on the 5.2 per cent growth recorded in 2024 and 2025 to achieve 5.4 per cent growth in the first quarter (1Q) of 2026. "At the same time, responsible fiscal management has enabled the government to cushion the impact of higher global fuel prices on the people and businesses through measures such as Budi Madani RON95 (BUDI95) and enhanced assistance under BUDI Diesel, while continuing to support household spending and economic growth," Anwar said in a statement released by the Finance Ministry (MoF).

items.title: Remand extended for drink driver over wrong-way crash, rider in ICU
items.summary: KLANG: The remand of a suspected drink driver who allegedly drove against traffic before crashing into a motorcycle along Persiaran Kota, leaving the rider critically injured, has been extended by four days until next Tuesday. Klang South district police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey, when contacted, said the victim, a man in his 20s, remained in critical condition at the intensive care unit (ICU) of Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital (HTAR). "Yes, the remand has been extended for four days until next Tuesday, May 19," he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] MOHE: Public university access move not recognition of other education systems
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Higher Education Ministry has stressed that the government's move to allow school leavers from outside the national education system to apply to public universities should not be viewed as recognition of other education systems. Its director-general Datuk Professor Dr Azlinda Azman said the move was aimed at widening access to education, not recognising any external system. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the move did not compromise the national education system , as it was aimed at widening access, not recognising other education systems within the national framework.

items.title: BYD, Sime Motors expand northern presence with Penang flagship hub
items.summary: BYD Malaysia and Sime Motors, the local distributor of BYD vehicles, are expanding showrooms, aftersales support and training programmes as they strengthen the electric vehicle (EV) ownership experience natuonwide. The companies currently operate more than 43 showrooms and over 25 dedicated aftersales centres, while also investing in local talent development and service capabilities to support the growing number of EV owners. Liu Xueliang, vice president of BYD Co Ltd and general manager of BYD Asia Pacific auto sales division, said the company sees long-term potential in Malaysia's EV market.

items.title: DBKL gets RM1.5m for 100 bus stops after roadside wait complaints, transport minister expects faster rollout than Penang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Ministry of Transport (MOT) approved an additional RM1.5 million allocation to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to construct and upgrade bus stops across the federal capital. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the move follows complaints from public transport users about the lack of proper bus facilities at several locations in Kuala Lumpur. Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah, and Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: MACC says no corruption found in KL flood retention pond land conversion probe after Hannah Yeoh query
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no elements of corruption in its investigation into the conversion of land status involving flood retention ponds in the capital, according to a report by Berita Harian . The probe centred on allegations of abuse of power and irregularities involving approvals for development projects near flood retention ponds in Kuala Lumpur, including in Taman Wahyu. “Among the allegations investigated was the conversion of the status of several areas near flood retention ponds in the capital, including in Taman Wahyu, for development purposes,” he reportedly said today.

items.title: Navy recovers 11th body from capsized Indonesian boat off Pulau Pangkor, three still missing
items.summary: IPOH, May 15 — Another body of a victim from a boat carrying Indonesian migrants that capsized off the waters of Pulau Pangkor was found this morning, bringing the number of recovered bodies to 11 so far. The Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said the body of a male victim was discovered by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) at 9.27 am, located 5.6 nautical miles from the incident site. The SAR operation involved seven assets, namely the Maritime Vessel KM Malawali (MMEA), Benteng 1203 (MMEA), KD Sri Indera Sakti (RMN), FCB 1001 (RMN), FCB 1030, PSC 16 (Marine Police) and PSC 24 (Marine Police), focusing on locating three more missing victims.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues severe weather warning for KL, Selangor and parts of Johor, Sabah till 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning affecting Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and nine states until 6pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Perak are Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. The warning also covers parts of Terengganu, namely Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman, as well as Pahang involving Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Bera and Rompin, in addition to Selangor.

items.title: Johor royal family attends Berandam Surai ceremony for youngest princess
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 15 — A Berandam Surai ceremony for the youngest daughter of Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, and his consort, Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Bustamam, was held at Istana Bukit Serene here today. Also present was the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail, and the other royal children. Tunku Khadeeja Khayra Iskandariah Tunku Ismail was born on April 5.

items.title: Must pass SPM BM and History first: Anwar confirms UEC students can enter public universities
items.summary: He said all students, regardless of their schooling stream, must meet the basic requirement of passing Bahasa Melayu and History subjects at the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) level. “The Cabinet decision on SMPC/UEC comes with the condition that students pass Bahasa Melayu and History subjects in SPM. “This does not apply only to SMPC/UEC students, but also to students from Arabic schools, secondary religious schools, tahfiz institutions and others.

items.title: College student, 24, faces murder charge over couple’s death in Jalan Ampang crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — A college student was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a married couple following a fatal crash along Jalan Ampang earlier this month. Danish Raman Kamar Kumaresan, 24, nodded in understanding after the charges were read before Magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri. The accused is alleged to have caused the deaths of A. Doraisingam, 61, and A. Menagah, 56, in a crash along Jalan Ampang towards Ampang Point from KLCC at 5.30 am on May 2.

items.title: On national Youth Day, Anwar announces RM5m allocation for Youth Council
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced a RM5 million allocation for the Malaysian Youth Council (MBM) to strengthen the umbrella body’s role in advancing youth development in the country. He said the additional RM1 million allocation compared with last year was recognition of MBM’s role as the main driver of the national youth agenda under the Ministry of Youth and Sports (KBS), while reflecting the government’s continued commitment to supporting youth development programmes. Also present were Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

items.title: New MACC chief Halim Aman vows stronger cooperation with AGC on high-profile cases
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will continue strengthening its strategic cooperation with the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC), particularly in handling high-profile, large-scale and public interest cases. Newly-appointed MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said such cases would continue to be referred to the AGC as the authority responsible for determining legal action on investigations conducted by the anti-graft agency. Meanwhile, Mohd Dusuki expressed appreciation for Abd Halim’s decision to make the AGC his first courtesy visit as the new MACC chief commissioner.

items.title: Jho Low pardon bid: Anwar says ‘we are not going that route’, leaves US decision to Washington
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia will not consider any pardon application involving fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low. Asked whether the government would formally oppose any move by the United States (US) regarding the matter, Anwar said: “As far as we are concerned, we are not going that route...but of course, what the US decides is their own business.” The Prime Minister also said that he was unaware of any pardon application submitted by Jho Low. According to the report, approval of the application would result in the US criminal charges against Jho Low being dropped.

items.title: Fiery crash involving trailer, van and car at Udini underpass leaves Penang traffic at standstill
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 15 — Three vehicles caught fire following a five-vehicle crash at the Udini Underpass on Jalan Masjid Negeri here today, causing severe traffic congestion. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said there were no reports of casualties. “Upon arrival, we found that the accident involved five vehicles, three of which were on fire.

items.title: Anthony Loke labels backlash against UEC public university access an attempt to politicise education
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today slammed criticism over the move to open public universities to graduates of Chinese Independent High Schools (SMPC) and Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) holders, describing it as an attempt to politicise education. Yesterday, the Higher Education Ministry (KPT) announced that students from outside the national education system, including UEC holders, would be allowed to enrol in public universities, marking a watershed decision as the UEC system has long been a flashpoint in race relations. He claimed that combining tahfiz graduates and UEC holders under the same announcement was a subtle attempt to legitimise the admission of UEC students into public universities.

items.title: Tiger teeth, bear fangs among RM5.32m wildlife parts seized in Kota Kinabalu raid
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 15 — Sabah wildlife authorities believe they have uncovered an illegal wildlife parts trading operation in the heart of the city following a raid on an antique shop along Jalan Gaya here two days ago. In a statement today, Sabah Wildlife Department director Soffian Abu Bakar said the raid uncovered several wildlife parts from protected species, including 16 tusks believed to be from wild boars, nine teeth believed to be from tigers, 10 teeth suspected to be from bears, various edible bird’s nest products of different weights, as well as 13 stones believed to be from porcupines. The case is being investigated under Sections 41(1) and 41(2) of the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for allegedly possessing, storing and displaying wildlife parts without valid permits.

items.title: RM57m in luxury houses, handbags, watches, cars seized as police crack international scam ring based in Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Police have arrested 187 suspects from nine countries in a major crackdown on international online scam syndicates operating out of the Klang Valley. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Khalid Ismail said the operation, dubbed Op Teguh 2.0, involved 46 raids on residential premises believed to be used as bases for cross-border scam activities. Khalid said the groups used several scam tactics, including fake investment platforms targeting victims in Hong Kong and China, impersonation scams involving fake Japanese police officers on Telegram, romance scams aimed at victims in Korea and Japan, and illegal online gambling operations.

items.title: Drugs, phone, weapon found inside Ipoh police lock-up, Bukit Aman orders full investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail has ordered a full investigation after drugs and other prohibited items were found inside a police lock-up in Ipoh, Perak. I have instructed district police chiefs to inspect lock-ups from time to time. “All state police chiefs have the authority to conduct inspections, and district police chiefs also carry out the same duties.

items.title: IGP confirms probe into claims fugitive Jho Low attended secret meetings in KL in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Investigations are ongoing into allegations that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, attended secret meetings in Kuala Lumpur last year, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said the investigations are strictly in accordance with existing legal procedures. “Therefore, investigations are carried out from time to time in accordance with the legal process permitted under the law,” he told reporters here.

items.title: Sarawak minister says 253 minors charged, four fined over smoking and vaping offences in first five months of 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Four minors were issued compounds for the illegal purchase of smoking products between January and May this year, said Sarawak Women, Childhood and Community Wellbeing Development Minister Datuk Seri Fatimah Abdullah. She said the offence was committed under Section 13(3) of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024. During the same period, she said 253 minors were charged under Section 17 (1) for smoking or vaping-related offences, while three vendors were charged for selling smoking products to minors under Section 13(1) of the same act.

items.title: Sarawak court sends 19-year-old to Henry Gurney School until 21 over child sexual abuse content
items.summary: KUCHING, May 15 — The Sessions Court here has ordered a 19-year-old student to be sent to Henry Gurney School Kuching until he reaches the age of 21 for having child sexual abuse materials on his smartphone. Judge Musli Ab Hamid also ordered the youth offender to undergo rehabilitative counselling throughout his detention period at Henry Gurney School Kuching in accordance with Section 26(1) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act (Soaca) 2017. The court further ordered the offender to undergo police supervision in Bau for two years upon his release from the institution pursuant to Section 27(1) of Soaca 2017.

items.title: Sabah Law Society backs state govt’s delay to federal border agency rollout over autonomy concerns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 15 — The Sabah Law Society (SLS) has expressed support for the state government’s decision to defer the implementation of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS), describing the move as necessary to safeguard Sabah’s constitutional rights and immigration autonomy. Its president Datuk Mohamed Nazim Maduarin said the State Cabinet’s decision demonstrated “legal foresight” by prioritising constitutional certainty over administrative expediency, particularly in matters involving Sabah’s special safeguards under the Federal Constitution and the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63). Nazim said the SLS concurred with the state government’s assessment that the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency Act 2024 (Act 860), in its current form, posed significant legal complexities in relation to Sabah’s autonomy.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh says MACC report on shrinking KL retention ponds lodged in 2021
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department Hannah Yeoh today revealed that a report over Kuala Lumpur’s shrinking flood retention ponds had already been lodged with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in 2021. “The report was made back in 2021 when the massive floods hit Kuala Lumpur. Three days ago, Yeoh ordered a halt to all development approvals involving land surrounding the Jinjang and Batu flood retention ponds after revealing the sites had shrunk to just 30 per cent of their original size.

items.title: With vape-related drug cases rising in Sarawak, minister says full ban still on the table
items.summary: KUCHING, May 15 — The Sarawak Government does not rule out imposing a complete ban on vaping in future, said Dato Sri Fatimah Abdullah. “The Sarawak Government believes that public awareness, prevention, and community readiness must first be strengthened to ensure that any future policy, including a possible complete ban, can be implemented effectively and receive wider public support,” she said. “Enforcement actions are being carried out in close collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police, through the Sarawak Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department, together with the Sarawak State Health Department.

items.title: Terengganu civil servant duped of almost RM100,000 in Telegram investment scams
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 15 — A female civil servant in Terengganu lost RM99,276 after falling victim to two fraudulent online investment schemes. Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 38-year-old victim responded to a ‘’Webull investment scheme’’ advertisement on the Telegram application on May 1, which promised lucrative returns within just six hours. “The deceived victim made 12 payments totalling RM48,965 into four different accounts between May 1 and 7,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms in northern peninsula, parts of Selangor, Johor and Sarawak until noon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to prevail until noon today in six states, according to the Meteorological Department. It said in a statement that the adverse weather will affect Perlis, Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang and Kuala Muda) as well as Penang. Similar conditions are expected in Kuala Selangor and Klang in Selangor; Batu Pahat, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru in Johor and Sibu, Mukah, Bintulu (Tatau and Bintulu) and Miri (Subis) in Sarawak.
